_meta.title: Medicare Inpatient Hospitals - by Geography and Service
_meta.vintage_note: CMS Medicare Inpatient by Geography & Service. Modified 2026-04-23 release. Underlying claims year: latest available FY (CMS releases lag 1-2 years; check landing_url for explicit year). Geographic granularity: 50 states + DC. Pricing semantics: avg_total_payment_usd = full episode payment (Medicare + secondary + patient share); avg_medicare_payment_usd = Medicare's share only. Patient out-of-pocket varies by Part A deductible and supplement coverage.

field_definitions.avg_total_payment_usd: Avg_Tot_Pymt_Amt — average total payment for the DRG (Medicare + secondary insurance + patient share)
field_definitions.avg_charge_usd: Avg_Submtd_Cvrd_Chrg — submitted (sticker) charge, typically 4-7x actual payment
field_definitions.n_discharges: Tot_Dschrgs — count of Medicare Part A inpatient discharges in the period
field_definitions.mapping_confidence: high = canonical DRG; medium = DRG family bundles related procedures; low = procedure mostly outpatient, inpatient cases are atypical

total-knee-replacement.drg_desc: MAJOR HIP AND KNEE JOINT REPLACEMENT OR REATTACHMENT OF LOWER EXTREMITY WITHOUT MCC
total-knee-replacement.mapping_note: DRG 470 bundles total knee and total hip replacement (CMS does not split them).

total-hip-replacement.drg_desc: MAJOR HIP AND KNEE JOINT REPLACEMENT OR REATTACHMENT OF LOWER EXTREMITY WITHOUT MCC
total-hip-replacement.mapping_note: DRG 470 bundles total hip and total knee replacement (CMS does not split them).

coronary-artery-bypass-graft-cabg.drg_desc: CORONARY BYPASS WITHOUT CARDIAC CATHETERIZATION WITH MCC
coronary-artery-bypass-graft-cabg.mapping_note: DRG 235: CABG without cardiac catheterization, with MCC. Most common Medicare CABG bucket.

heart-valve-replacement.drg_desc: CARDIAC VALVE AND OTHER MAJOR CARDIOTHORACIC PROCEDURES WITH CARDIAC CATHETERIZATION WIT
heart-valve-replacement.mapping_note: DRG 216: cardiac valve & other major cardiothoracic procedure with cardiac cath, with MCC.

spinal-fusion.drg_desc: SPINAL FUSION EXCEPT CERVICAL WITHOUT MCC
spinal-fusion.mapping_note: DRG 460: spinal fusion except cervical, without MCC. Most common Medicare bucket.

defibrillator-implantation-icd.mapping_note: DRG 277: cardiac defibrillator implant without MCC. Highest-volume ICD bucket.

pacemaker-implantation.drg_desc: PERMANENT CARDIAC PACEMAKER IMPLANT WITHOUT CC/MCC
pacemaker-implantation.mapping_note: DRG 244: permanent cardiac pacemaker implant without CC/MCC.

cardiac-catheterization-with-stent.drg_desc: PERCUTANEOUS CARDIOVASCULAR PROCEDURES WITH INTRALUMINAL DEVICE WITHOUT MCC
cardiac-catheterization-with-stent.mapping_note: DRG 322: percutaneous cardiovascular procedures with intraluminal device, without MCC. Most common Medicare PCI-with-stent bucket.

sepsis-treatment.drg_desc: SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITH MCC
sepsis-treatment.mapping_note: DRG 871: septicemia or severe sepsis without MV >96 hours, with MCC. Highest-volume sepsis DRG.

hip-fracture-repair.drg_desc: HIP AND FEMUR PROCEDURES EXCEPT MAJOR JOINT WITH CC
hip-fracture-repair.mapping_note: DRG 481: hip & femur procedures except major joint, with CC.

stroke-treatment-acute.drg_desc: INTRACRANIAL HEMORRHAGE OR CEREBRAL INFARCTION WITH CC OR TPA IN 24 HOURS
stroke-treatment-acute.mapping_note: DRG 065: intracranial hemorrhage or cerebral infarction with CC or tPA in 24h.

heart-failure-treatment-inpatient.drg_desc: HEART FAILURE AND SHOCK WITH MCC
heart-failure-treatment-inpatient.mapping_note: DRG 291: heart failure & shock, with MCC. Highest-volume HF DRG.

pneumonia-treatment-inpatient.drg_desc: SIMPLE PNEUMONIA AND PLEURISY WITH MCC
pneumonia-treatment-inpatient.mapping_note: DRG 193: simple pneumonia & pleurisy, with MCC.

copd-exacerbation-treatment.drg_desc: CHRONIC OBSTRUCTIVE PULMONARY DISEASE WITH CC
copd-exacerbation-treatment.mapping_note: DRG 191: chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, with CC.

colectomy.drg_desc: MAJOR SMALL AND LARGE BOWEL PROCEDURES WITH CC
colectomy.mapping_note: DRG 330: major small & large bowel procedures, with CC.

gallbladder-removal-cholecystectomy.drg_desc: LAPAROSCOPIC CHOLECYSTECTOMY WITHOUT C.D.E. WITH CC
gallbladder-removal-cholecystectomy.mapping_note: DRG 418: laparoscopic cholecystectomy without C.D.E., with CC.

hernia-repair.drg_desc: INGUINAL AND FEMORAL HERNIA PROCEDURES WITH CC
hernia-repair.mapping_note: DRG 351: inguinal & femoral hernia procedures, with CC. Most common hernia type in Medicare population.

mastectomy.mapping_note: DRG 582: mastectomy for malignancy, with CC/MCC. Medicare mastectomy volume is modest; many state cells suppressed.

laminectomy.drg_desc: BACK AND NECK PROCEDURES EXCEPT SPINAL FUSION WITH CC
laminectomy.mapping_note: DRG 519: back & neck procedures except spinal fusion, with CC. CMS bundles laminectomy and lumbar disc procedures into the same DRG family.

lumbar-disc-surgery.drg_desc: BACK AND NECK PROCEDURES EXCEPT SPINAL FUSION WITH CC
lumbar-disc-surgery.mapping_note: DRG 519: back & neck procedures except spinal fusion, with CC. CMS bundles laminectomy and lumbar disc procedures into the same DRG family.

prostatectomy.mapping_note: DRG 713: transurethral prostatectomy (TURP), with CC/MCC. Most common prostatectomy type in Medicare population.

hysterectomy.drg_desc: UTERINE AND ADNEXA PROCEDURES FOR NON-MALIGNANCY WITH CC/MCC
hysterectomy.mapping_note: DRG 742: uterine & adnexa procedure for non-malignancy, with CC.

cesarean-section.drg_desc: CESAREAN SECTION WITHOUT STERILIZATION WITHOUT CC/MCC
cesarean-section.mapping_note: DRG 788: cesarean section without sterilization, without CC/MCC. Medicare rarely covers obstetric care (Medicaid is the primary payer); most state cells suppressed.

rotator-cuff-repair.drg_desc: SHOULDER, ELBOW OR FOREARM PROCEDURES, EXCEPT MAJOR JOINT PROCEDURES WITH CC
rotator-cuff-repair.mapping_note: DRG 511: shoulder, elbow or forearm procedures (except major joint), with CC. Most rotator cuff repairs are outpatient; inpatient covers complex/complication cases only.

acl-reconstruction.drg_desc: KNEE PROCEDURES WITHOUT PRINCIPAL DIAGNOSIS OF INFECTION WITH CC/MCC
acl-reconstruction.mapping_note: DRG 488: knee procedures without principal diagnosis of infection, with CC/MCC. ACL reconstruction is usually outpatient; inpatient covers complex/comorbid cases.

cataract-surgery-inpatient.mapping_note: DRG 117: intraocular procedures without CC/MCC. >95% of cataract surgery is outpatient; this DRG covers rare inpatient cases (e.g. complex comorbidity).

liver-transplant.drg_desc: LIVER TRANSPLANT WITH MCC OR INTESTINAL TRANSPLANT
liver-transplant.mapping_note: DRG 005: liver transplant with MCC or intestinal transplant.
